_source: Five Parsecs From Home: Planetfall pp.21-25
_description: Character background tables: Motivation (all chars), Prior Experience (experienced only), Notable Events (experienced only, narrative flavor)

entries.notes: If you didn't go, someone else would get to see all the cool stuff instead.
Promotion, glory, or a place in the history books.
Doing your duty for your organization.
It's not a death-wish if you survive.
Striking out on your own.
This is where you ended up.
The advancement of the Human species.
Excitement, amazing sights, and thrills.
Mapping out the galaxy one world at a time.
Being part of something much greater than yourself.
Getting away from something or someone.
Fulfilling a responsibility, promise, or expectation.

prior_experience_table._note: D100, rolled for 4 experienced characters only. Stat bonuses added to starting profile.

entries.notes: Unity armed forces or a mercenary outfit
Salvage missions, questionable jobs, and a bit of light mercenary work
Science, information gathering, and analysis (often corporate in nature)
Independent space merchant or sector-level corporation
Raised and given an education by Unity
Affiliated with one of the many clandestine covert operations programs maintained by Unity
Served in one of the Bug Hunting squads; flinches when motion trackers beep
Dutiful work in sector administration or the Unity Bureaucracy itself
Worked for one of the giga-corporations
Independent explorer or part of the Unity scout fleet
Often driven by thrills and the promise of riches
No records found: please contact sector-level Enforcement Command for details
Sector-level law enforcement or Unity Security
Officer or enlisted on a Unity military vessel
Please insert Unity ID chip of security level 5Z or adjacent. Unauthorized access is prohibited.

notable_event_table._note: D100, rolled for experienced characters only. No gameplay effect — narrative flavor only.

entries.notes: You went somewhere far from home.
You established or expanded your family, adopted or otherwise.
Someone betrayed you or you betrayed someone.
You made great strides as a person.
A dreadful disaster struck you, your organization, or your home.
You were seriously injured or sick.
You joined an organization, community, or philosophical movement.
Life changed how you look at things.
You spent a period of time when nobody knew where you were.
Without expecting a reward, you did the right thing.
You decided to learn a new skill or career.
You did nothing but you got blamed regardless.
You screwed up and it got noticed.
You made advances in your career.
Something lucky and unlikely happened.
You had a showdown with a rival or threat.
You experienced great physical danger.
Once arrested, destitute, or nearly dead, you eventually came out ahead.
Someone gave you a second chance.
You changed something fundamental about your lifestyle.
